meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve No Portfolio (Checklist)
content.seo_description: Bridge the gap between no portfolio and passive income. A high-impact guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch AI SaaS projects.
content.answer_block: Transform your 'no portfolio' status into a revenue-generating asset. By deploying micro-SaaS tools using Antigravity and Claude Code, you demonstrate real-world engineering capability while proving market viability. This checklist bridges the gap between academic theory and high-value, passive income-generating software that catches recruiters' attention immediately.
content.intro:
The silence of a blank GitHub repository is the loudest sound in a developer's career. You have the skills, you understand the theory, but you lack the 'battle scars' of a live product. Recruiters don't want to see another To-Do list app; they want to see someone who can navigate the chaotic intersection of code, deployment, and user acquisition.

Today, we stop building for grades and start building for the market. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architecture and Claude Code for autonomous implementation, you will launch a functional, revenue-ready AI tool. This isn't just a project—it’s a digital storefront that proves you can build, scale, and monetize in the real world.

steps.title: Define a High-Utility Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a specific, painful bottleneck in a niche industry. Focus on a tool that saves time, not just one that looks cool.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a SaaS architect. Generate 5 unique micro-SaaS ideas that solve specific pain points for remote project managers. For each, define the target audience, the core value prop, and the tech stack.
steps.claude_code_command: claude research 'market gaps in productivity software for freelancers'

steps.title: Architect the MVP with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to scaffold your project structure, ensuring best practices for scalability and modularity from day one.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack Next.js application using Tailwind CSS and Supabase. Include authentication, a stripe integration hook, and a clean folder structure for AI agent integration.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'scaffold project using Antigravity schema'

steps.title: Implement the Core AI Logic
steps.description: Use Claude Code to integrate your AI logic, focusing on prompt engineering and efficient API calls to keep latency low.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust service class in TypeScript that handles OpenAI API calls with retry logic, streaming responses, and structured JSON output.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'implement streaming chat service with error handling'

steps.title: Automate the Monetization Layer
steps.description: Integrate Stripe to handle payments, transforming your code from a 'project' into a 'business'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe subscription webhook handler in Next.js that updates the user's database record to 'premium' upon successful payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'configure stripe webhook and subscription state management'

steps.title: Deploy and Portfolio-Optimise
steps.description: Deploy to Vercel and finalize your README to highlight the engineering challenges you solved, not just the features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md that highlights the system architecture, the technical challenges faced, and the metrics of the MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude publish 'deploy to production and commit README'

pro_tips: Always include a 'Lessons Learned' section in your README; hiring managers care more about how you debugged a race condition than the code itself.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API routes before you even write the UI; it forces you to think about the contract first.
Focus on one specific 'micro-niche' (e.g., AI tools for dog groomers) rather than a general-purpose tool to make marketing easier.
Set up automated error tracking (like Sentry) early; it shows you understand the operational reality of managing live software.
Don't aim for perfection; aim for 'shippable.' A live, buggy app is worth 100x more than a perfect, local-only project.
content.next_steps: Once your app is live, share it on Twitter (X) and IndieHackers. Your goal is to get your first 10 users—not to make a million dollars—to prove the loop works.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer a student; you are an AI Builder with a live, revenue-generating product. Stop waiting for permission to be an engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
